assertions.title: Each child has a pre-completed Philomena Protocol profile ready for police sharing

steps.action: Open the created child profile and inspect the visible tabs/actions.
steps.expected: A user-facing Philomena profile entry point is discoverable from the child profile.
steps.observed: The child profile exposed Overview, Care Plans, Care Notes, Risk Assessments, Documents, Body Map, Care Package, Clinical, EMAR, Incidents, Keyworker, and Timeline only; no Philomena or missing-from-care entry point was visible.

steps.action: Open the safeguarding dashboard and inspect available safeguarding workflows.
steps.expected: A Philomena or missing-from-care workflow is reachable from safeguarding.
steps.observed: The safeguarding page showed only Record Concern, MASH Referrals, LADO Referrals, and Section 47 Investigations; no Philomena or missing-from-care option was present.

steps.action: Open the admissions list and new referral form.
steps.expected: The admission/referral flow should expose or prompt the Philomena profile required on admission.
steps.observed: Admissions and the new referral form contained no Philomena-related control, prompt, or handover/print action.

steps.action: Attempt a plausible child route for the Philomena profile (`/persons/{personId}/philomena`).
steps.expected: The route should open the Philomena profile with printable police-handover details.
steps.observed: The route returned a 404 page (`This page could not be found`).

assertions.issues: No discoverable Philomena profile surface exists in the real UI, so the required profile fields, printable/exportable police handover page, and photo-age alert could not be reached or verified.

assertions.title: Missing episode recorded with timestamped escalation steps

steps.action: Inspect the child profile for any missing-episode action or section.
steps.expected: Staff should be able to start a Missing Episode record from the child profile or related child workflow.
steps.observed: No missing-episode action, button, tab, or chronology shortcut was visible on the child profile.

steps.action: Inspect the safeguarding dashboard for a missing-from-care workflow.
steps.expected: Safeguarding should expose missing-episode recording and escalation controls.
steps.observed: Safeguarding exposed concern/referral flows only; no missing-episode recording, timer, or escalation path was available.

steps.action: Inspect admissions and referral surfaces for a missing-from-care pathway tied to child admission/setup.
steps.expected: If missing workflows are admission-linked, they should be discoverable from the admissions surface.
steps.observed: Admissions surfaces contained no missing-from-care controls or escalation configuration.

steps.action: Attempt a plausible child route for missing episodes (`/persons/{personId}/missing-from-care`).
steps.expected: The route should open a missing-episode list or creation flow.
steps.observed: The route returned a 404 page (`This page could not be found`).

assertions.issues: The real UI does not expose a Missing Episode creation or escalation surface, so staff cannot record absence, trigger timestamped escalation steps, or record the child's return.

assertions.title: RHI created on child's return with 72-hour compliance deadline

steps.action: Inspect the child profile and safeguarding surfaces for any Return Home Interview task, countdown, or compliance card.
steps.expected: A user-facing RHI task/workflow should be discoverable after a missing episode or from safeguarding/child views.
steps.observed: No RHI entry point, task, countdown, or overdue indicator was visible on the child profile or safeguarding dashboard.

steps.action: Inspect the admissions surfaces for any RHI or missing-return compliance workflow.
steps.expected: If linked from child admissions/setup, RHI controls should appear in the admissions flow.
steps.observed: Admissions and the new referral form contained no RHI or return-home workflow.

steps.action: Attempt a plausible direct child route for RHI (`/persons/{personId}/rhi`).
steps.expected: The route should open the Return Home Interview task/form.
steps.observed: The route returned a 404 page (`This page could not be found`).

assertions.issues: No Return Home Interview task or route is exposed in the real UI, so the required auto-created RHI, 72-hour countdown, escalation to the Responsible Individual, and chronology linkage cannot be performed.

assertions.title: Approved contacts register with court order restrictions enforced

steps.expected: The approved contacts register should display contact rows or provide a working form to add contacts with relationship, type, supervision, restrictions, and court-order metadata.
steps.observed: The page loaded an empty placeholder register with tabs and an `Add Contact` button, but no existing contacts or fields for restrictions/court-order details were present.

steps.expected: A form or modal should open to create an approved contact and capture restrictions/court-order conditions.
steps.observed: No dialog opened (`dialogCount: 0`), the URL stayed on `/contacts`, and the page remained on the same empty-state message (`No approved contacts registered for this child.`).

steps.action: Switch to `Scheduled Visits` and probe scheduling from the register.
steps.expected: Scheduling should validate against approved contacts, block unapproved/restricted contacts, and allow manager override with recorded justification when required.
steps.observed: The Scheduled Visits tab was another empty shell; `Schedule Visit` opened no form and there was no blocked-alert, restriction check, court-order UI, or manager-override capture available anywhere on the page.

evidence.network: GET /child-core-c-home/persons/19af8676-07eb-4d6b-bc1c-711f232dc720/contacts -> 200; no POST/PUT requests were emitted when `Add Contact` or `Schedule Visit` were clicked

assertions.issues: The contact-management UI is only a placeholder shell: approved contacts cannot be added, court-order restrictions cannot be entered, and the required scheduling enforcement/manager override workflow is absent.

assertions.title: Contact visits scheduled and recorded with child's emotional presentation

steps.action: Switch to `Scheduled Visits` and inspect the available scheduling controls.
steps.expected: Staff should be able to schedule a contact visit with date/time/duration/contact type/supervision data.
steps.observed: The tab showed only an empty-state message and a `Schedule Visit` button; clicking the button opened no form (`dialogCount: 0`) and left the page unchanged.

steps.action: Switch to `Contact Records` and inspect the recording workflow.
steps.expected: Staff should be able to record the completed contact with who attended, emotional presentation before/during/after, concerns, and disclosures.
steps.observed: The tab showed only an empty-state message and a `Record Contact` button; clicking the button opened no form (`dialogCount: 0`) and left the page unchanged.

steps.action: Switch to `Compliance` and inspect the tracker.
steps.expected: The page should show achieved-vs-planned contact frequency (for example, `Weekly phone with mum: 4/4 this month`).
steps.observed: The Compliance tab rendered only the empty-state message `No approved contacts to track compliance for.` with no compliance metrics or planned-vs-achieved tracker.

evidence.network: GET /child-core-c-home/persons/19af8676-07eb-4d6b-bc1c-711f232dc720/contacts -> 200; no POST/PUT requests were emitted when `Schedule Visit` or `Record Contact` were clicked

assertions.issues: The page exposes labels for scheduling, recording, and compliance only; the actual contact-visit workflow is not implemented in the user-facing UI.

frictions.description: The onboarding flow ignored the manually entered slug `childcore-c-home` and redirected the new organisation to `child-core-c-home`, so the supplied route hints initially 404'd until I followed the actual post-onboarding URL.
frictions.resolution: Used the slug shown in the live dashboard URL (`child-core-c-home`) for all subsequent browser checks while keeping the same isolated org/account.

summary: Tested 5 assertions: 0 passed, 5 failed. Missing-from-care/Philomena/RHI surfaces are absent from the real browser UI, and the child contact-management route is exposed only as an empty placeholder shell with inert action buttons.
